page: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a single thread per core configuration. The CPU allocation is shared, which introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs compared to dedicated instances. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 150 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core testing placing the instance in the top 25% of its class at 2938.58. Multi-core performance aligns with the middle 50% average, yielding a stress-ng multi-core score of 23323.12 and a PassMark CPU score of 18066.14. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also fall within the average range, recording 1047.13 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 123.16 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For cryptographic and compression tasks, the server demonstrates average capabilities, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA-NI instruction sets.

While the instance handles small-scale language model tasks adequately, processing a medium 7B LLM model reveals a performance bottleneck, with prompt processing speeds dropping to a weak 25.18 tokens/sec. However, text generation for the same 7B model remains average at 15.99 tokens/sec. Given the shared CPU allocation and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server represents a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high single-core burst capacity but can tolerate shared resource environments. It is suitable for static web serving, redis caching, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: The Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 150 GB NVMe SSD, and a shared CPU allocation. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. The instance includes hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX, AES, and SHA_NI. However, resource sharing presents a tradeoff, and LLM prompt processing for medium 7B models is relatively weak. This server is suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and redis caching where a balanced 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio is required.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome server with 8 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 150 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and database tasks.

tagline: An AMD EPYC-powered instance with shared vCPUs, bundled NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for balanced computing workloads.
